what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry that documents and evaluates a generated oxlint configuration named _adherence.oxlintrc.json, describing its design-system enforcement rules, embedded token manifest, and observed generation artefacts.
observations: The file is a structured interpretive JSON for the claude-ds project, produced by model opus.
It summarizes an oxlint config that forbids design-system internal imports, raw hex colors, raw px values, and non-system fonts.
It lists per-component JSXOpeningElement selectors that whitelist allowed props and restrict prop values to enumerated sets.
All lint rules are set to 'warn' rather than 'error'.
An overrides block disables no-restricted-imports for **/index.js, the allowed barrel re-export.
A non-standard 'x-omelette' block carries a components map, a flat list of ~470 design tokens, a tokenKinds classification map, and a fontFamilies list.
Every component entry in the x-omelette components map has an empty 'replaces' array.
The interpretive layer itself flags mismatched tokenKinds classifications and an empty react/forbid-elements rule.
standouts: The lint config doubles as a machine-readable token manifest by embedding ~470 tokens and a token-kind map inside an x-omelette extension.
Component API contracts — allowed props plus enumerated values — are encoded directly as oxlint AST selectors.
All rules are 'warn', so the config reports drift without failing builds on its own.
Several tokenKinds entries appear misclassified (e.g. spacing-like tokens marked 'color'), suggesting classifier artefacts.
conventions: Generated-not-hand-edited: the summarized config is treated as compiled output.
Token-driven styling: raw hex, px, and non-system fonts are rejected in favor of var() tokens.
Single-entry import discipline: consumers must import from index.js, not component internals.
Closed enumerations: component props and their values are whitelisted and lint-restricted.
Extension-namespace pattern: x-omelette carries registry metadata inside a standard config schema.
Interpretive ledger schema: uses fields like observations, standouts, concerns, questions, and contribution to annotate a substrate.
concerns: react/forbid-elements is configured with an empty forbid list, making it a no-op rule.
Every component's 'replaces' array is empty, so a declared replacement/migration field is unpopulated.
tokenKinds contains classifications that look wrong for several tokens (e.g. desktop width/gutter tokens marked 'color').
All rules are 'warn', so the config does not enforce adherence by failing the build.
The interpretive layer itself cannot verify whether the embedded token catalogue matches the actual source-of-truth registry.
notes: An interpretive summary of a generated oxlint config that encodes design-system import discipline, no-raw-literal rules, and per-component prop/value contracts while also carrying the full token catalogue and several likely generation artefacts.
questions: What tool generates the _adherence.oxlintrc.json file and its x-omelette block?
Why are all rules set to 'warn' instead of 'error' if the goal is strict adherence?
Are the tokenKinds entries that classify spacing/width-like tokens as 'color' intentional or a generation bug?
What is the intended use of the empty 'replaces' arrays in the x-omelette components map?
Why is react/forbid-elements included with an empty forbid list?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as an interpretive ledger entry for an adherence-enforcing oxlint config, and its contents align with that purpose.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to make design-system drift lint-detectable by encoding import boundaries, no-raw-literal rules, and per-component prop/value contracts into an oxlint config, and to use that same config as a canonical vocabulary manifest via the x-omelette extension.
novelty: It is unusual to embed a complete token catalogue with kind classification and full component API contracts inside an oxlint config through a custom extension key, so that the lint config also serves as the design-system manifest.
contribution: It contributes a machine-readable adherence specification: import-discipline rules, raw-literal prohibitions, per-component allowed props and enumerated values, and a flat token catalogue with kind classification that a fused system could query or enforce against.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger entry for a generated oxlint config that enforces design-system adherence through import restrictions, raw-literal bans, and per-component prop/value AST selectors, while embedding a ~470-token catalogue and token-kind map in an x-omelette extension block.
intention_for_embedding: To document and expose the design system's machine-enforceable contracts and canonical token vocabulary as a lint-config-cum-manifest.
